description_long: This is a standard manual screwdriver featuring a cylindrical handle with a red and yellow color scheme, likely made of plastic for durability and grip. The handle is smooth and straight, with subtle faceting for improved ergonomics and control during use. Extending from the handle is a long, slender metallic shaft, which appears to be made of steel, culminating in a flathead tip suitable for slotted screws. The shaft is dark in color, contrasting with the bright handle, and is securely embedded into the handle. The overall design is utilitarian, typical for hand tools found in garages, workshops, or urban maintenance settings. No visible branding or markings are present, and the object appears to be in pristine condition with no signs of wear or damage.
description: A red and yellow straight-handled screwdriver with a long, dark metallic shaft and a flathead tip. The handle is smooth and faceted, and the tool appears new and undamaged.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow end of the handle and the shaft extending directly away, with the red and yellow handle facets visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the screwdriver, highlighting the cylindrical handle and the long, thin metallic shaft with a flathead tip.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the shaft end-on and the full width of the handle, with the handle's faceted design visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the handle and shaft profile from the other side.
